emails.subject: Re: Quarterly Financial Review
emails.body_summary: Jordan raised concerns about some large unrecorded expenses in Q1.

meetings.title: Year-End Financial Assessment
meetings.transcript_summary: Discussed incomplete entries in financial systems and potential data sources for corrections.
meetings.description: Review and address financial discrepancies from the past year.

commitments.description: Complete the reconciliation of mismatched financial entries by the end of Q2

operational_insights.title: Unmatched Entry: Acme Office Supplies
operational_insights.summary: The entry for $1200 with Acme Office Supplies on Jan 5, 2026, does not match recorded expenses.
operational_insights.recommended_action: Verify all purchases from Acme Office Supplies and update records accordingly.
operational_insights.explanation: Transaction appears in payment data but not in internal expense logs.

operational_insights.title: Potential Duplicate Software Payment
operational_insights.summary: Existence of software expense matching $550 for Tech licenses could indicate a duplicate.
operational_insights.recommended_action: Investigate potential duplicate transaction across categories.
operational_insights.explanation: Transaction from Global Software Distribution could overlap with payments to Tech World previously.

actual_relationships: Jordan is an external consultant specializing in financial oversight for StartupX.
Alex Johnson oversees all financial records for StartupX.
hidden_ground_truth.actual_intent: Retrieve all mismatched entries between transaction records and operational insights for 2026.
expected_answer_facts: Mismatch entry for $1200 from Acme Office Supplies does not align with operational records.
Both $550 transactions from Tech World and Global Software Distribution potentially overlap; requires investigation.
